title: fragment.rs: FragmentReceiver does not validate rule_id consistency across fragments
description:
File: rust/lichen-schc/src/fragment.rs
Lines: 507-565 (FragmentReceiver::receive)

Category: PROTOCOL

The FragmentReceiver updates rule_id on every fragment received:
```rust
pub fn receive(&mut self, frag: &Fragment<'_>) -> ReceiverResult {
    ...
    self.rule_id = frag.rule_id;  // Line 515
    ...
}
```

If fragments from different datagrams (with different rule_ids) are interleaved or mixed up, the receiver:
1. Accepts them all into the same reassembly buffer
2. Uses the last-seen rule_id for the ACK
3. May produce a corrupt reassembled datagram

Per RFC 8724 section 8.4.2, the receiver should maintain per-rule_id state or reject fragments with mismatched rule_ids.

Impact: In multi-flow scenarios or under attack, mismatched fragments could be incorrectly reassembled, producing corrupt data that passes MIC check only by chance.

Fix: Either:
1. Store the rule_id from the first fragment and reject subsequent fragments with different rule_ids
2. Or maintain separate reassembly state per rule_id
